$description: Liquid/Jinja grammar REFERENCE CHECKLIST for the #2113 grammar-fidelity scorecard — WE-owned data, not scorer code. One shared bundle for both flavours: Liquid and Jinja share the same delimiter skeleton ({{ }} + {% %} + {# #}); their divergence is expression vocabulary (filters/tags), not grammar, so the delimiter surface is identical — one checklist covers both. Fork 3 stress: the end-prefix name-echo close ({% for %}…{% endfor %}, {% if %}…{% endif %}) is the SECOND declared-close shape in the corpus alongside the {/each} form. {% raw %}…{% endraw %} is the mandatory verbatim escape hatch. {# #} (Jinja) / {% comment %}…{% endcomment %} (Liquid) are the dual hidden-comment paths. Scored against BUNDLE ZERO (FUI's native mustache+polymer recipes, frontierui:plugs/webnodes/recipes/interpolationRecipes.ts) the gap list seeds the Liquid/Jinja bundle story (#2115). Each construct names its opening delimiter + expected #2074 nature; outOfScope marks delimiter-grammar concerns #2074 does NOT own. The scorer (frontierui:plugs/webnodes/grammarScorecard.ts scoreGrammar) grades each construct reproduced/partial/out-of-scope/gap. Zero scorer logic here — swapping this for another framework's checklist is what proves the scorer framework-agnostic (#2113).

constructs.outOfScope: expression vocabulary (filter/pipe syntax) — not a delimiter-grammar concern #2074 owns
expression vocabulary (template inclusion tags) — not a delimiter-grammar construct; the delimiter {%...%} is shared, the include semantics are tag-specific
